items.claim: Benoit Saint-Denis fights by walking opponents down on a straight line.
items.quote: heavy southpaw striker, likes to just walk you down on a straight line

items.claim: Benoit Saint-Denis is highly aggressive with double-leg takedowns regardless of submission risks.
items.quote: will shoot the double with complete disregard to getting his neck caught in any submissions

items.claim: Benoit Saint-Denis possesses high-level top pressure and positional awareness on the ground.
items.quote: Once he gets you on the ground, he's so heavy on top. He's got heavy hips. He's got good positioning

items.claim: Paddy Pimblett's grappling level is superior to Dan Hooker's.
items.quote: Patty Pimpblelet's grappling is on a different level.

items.claim: Benoit Saint-Denis lacks the Division I wrestling background required to control Paddy Pimblett in a front headlock.
items.quote: he didn't wrestle for division one college. You're not going to be able to head snap Patty Pimpblelet down on all fours and control him in a front headlock

items.claim: Paddy Pimblett has a larger physical frame than Dan Hooker.
items.quote: Patty Pimpblelet's a lot bigger than he is... The bone structure of these two guys is completely different.

items.claim: Paddy Pimblett's chin is potentially compromised due to the damage taken in his fight against Justin Gaethje less than seven months ago.
items.quote: Is the chin compromised? You know, did he not take enough time off? That could play a factor in this fight.

items.claim: Conor McGregor possesses the best check cross in UFC history.
items.quote: The best check cross I have ever seen in the UFC. The best check cross I've ever seen in my life was actually the one against Jose Aldo.

items.claim: Conor McGregor's long layoff and focus on business ventures have likely hindered his ability to evolve his fighting style.
items.quote: it's hard for me to believe that this guy truly has evolved his style... he's got so many business ventures he's got a lot of things going on in his life

items.claim: Max Holloway is a volume puncher who utilizes stance changes and lateral footwork to compensate for a shorter reach.
items.quote: Max Holloway very much a volume puncher through and through... he does such a good job boxing his way through his stance changes, taking angles, using lateral footwork
